# montest

Tools for detecting violations of monotonicity and LATE assumptions using machine learning.

## Installation

You can install the development version from GitHub:

```r
install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("martin-andresen/montest")
```

## Overview

`montest` searches for violations of monotonicity and LATE assumptions in subsets of the data and for various margins of the instrument, treatment (and outcome) using sample splitting and machine learning methods (e.g. generalized random forests and CART).

`montestplot` provides visualization tools for the detected violations.

## Example

```r
library(montest)

out <- montest(
  data = mydata,
  D = "D",
  Z = "Z",
  X = c("x1", "x2"),
  test = "simple"
)

montestplot(out$forest)
```

## Main functions

- `montest()` — detects violations of monotonicity / LATE assumptions
- `montestplot()` — visualizes results

## Methodology

The package implements data-adaptive tests for monotonicity and LATE assumptions based on:
- sample splitting
- cross-fitting
- machine learning subset search

## Status

This is a research package under active development. Details are based on: "Testing the Monotonicity Assumption in Instrumental Variable Models", working paper by Martin Eckhoff Andresen, Martin Huber and Tymon Słoczyński.
